titleStmt.title: Letter from J. Houlihan to Sir Robert Chalmers, 8 June 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: J. Houlihan

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This letter was addressed to the Under-Secretary for Ireland, Sir Robert Chalmers (1858-1938). Chalmers was sent to Ireland as under-secretary, replacing Sir Matthew Nathan who had resigned along with Augustine Birrell , his chief secretary following the Easter Rising in 1916. The letter concerns the prosecution of Mr William Hastings for non-compliance with the provisions of the National Insurance Acts.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/9743

correspAction.placeName: National Health insurance Commission, Dublin, Ireland.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
National Health Insurance Commission,
Dublin. 8th June 1916.
No. 46,060/1915. Sir, I am directed by the National Health Insurance
Commission (Ireland) to state, for the information of
His Excellency the Lord Lieutenant, that a prosecution
for non-compliance with the provisions of the National
Insurance Acts has been instituted against Mr.William
Hastings of Ballinasloe and the case is fixed for
hearing on the 17th instant. Our local Solicitor is
of the opinion that it would be desirable, if possible,
to have a Resident Magistrate in attendance at the
Ballinasloe Petty Sessions on the day named and he has
requested this Commission to make representatives to
that effect to the Lord Lieutenant. I am, Sir,
Your obedient Servant, John Houlihan The Under Secretary,
Dublin Castle.
